prompt:
Vertical 9:16 cinematic devotional oil painting video clip, slower pacing with longer holds and restrained cuts.

Classical devotional oil painting realism. Warm light rests on an inclined face crowned with thorns while the cross beam and the surrounds sink into deep shadow; visible brushwork, craquelure, aged varnish throughout.

Scene contains:
A close reverent view of the crucified Christ's face, the head inclined, the eyes lowered with tender, willing love rather than pain, a crown of thorns upon the brow, faint warm light on one cheek against deep shadow, the grain of the cross beam at his shoulder and a fold of cream robe. The scene is a painted tableau — a frozen moment, not an action. Nothing moves; this is a still painting.

Scene:
Start on full composition — the inclined face beneath the crown of thorns.

Cut to close-up — the lowered, loving eyes.

Cut to insert — the crown of thorns on the brow.

Cut to insert — a single thorn against the skin.

Cut to macro detail — warm light on one cheek.

Cut to insert — the cream robe and cross beam at the shoulder.

Return to full composition.

End hold on the tender, inclined face.

Micro-motion:
- minimal

Camera:
- crop-based reframing only
- no invented movement

Emotion:
Tender, willing love.

No added elements.

10.0s

9:16
negative_prompt: no camera invention, no zoom invented, no pan invented, no push-in invented, no dolly, no tracking, no rotation, no animated subject motion, no figures moving, no morphing, no scene morphing, no facial morphing, no glow added, no light rays added beyond what is painted, no atmospheric haze, no scene enhancement, no divine effects added, no smooth transitions, no motion blur, no reinterpretation, no new elements, no text, no watermarks, no extra limbs, no flickering, no warping, no animation, no action, no subject performs any task, no body motion, no limbs moving, the painting is fixed and frozen, only the camera reframes within the still image

beats.description: Start on full composition — inclined face beneath the crown of thorns
Cut to close-up — the lowered, loving eyes
Cut to insert — the crown of thorns on the brow
Cut to insert — a single thorn against the skin
Cut to macro detail — warm light on one cheek
Cut to insert — cream robe and cross beam at the shoulder
Return to full composition, end hold on the tender inclined face
